title: Data Representation
description: How computers store numbers, text, images and sound as binary: number bases, binary arithmetic, two's complement negative numbers, floating-point, and character/image/sound encoding.
objectives: Convert numbers between binary, denary and hexadecimal
Add binary numbers of a fixed bit-width and identify when overflow occurs
Represent and interpret negative integers using two's complement for a fixed bit-width
Explain fixed-point and simplified floating-point (sign, mantissa, exponent) representation of real numbers
Explain how character sets (ASCII, extended ASCII, Unicode/UTF-8) encode text as binary
Calculate the storage requirements of simple images and sound from resolution, colour depth, sample rate and bit depth
